narrative:
We are in a stiff WNW flow today. Windy conditions (especially through early afternoon) are the main takeaway from today's weather, but temperatures are subtly warmer. Windy conditions combined with our ongoing drought make this the most dangerous day we have had for wildfires—caution is required. Thursday and Friday are pleasant—the picks of the week weather-wise. An approaching front brings showers Saturday afternoon into the evening.

This week's Monday Message addresses the temperature roller-coaster, drought status, and improving rain prospects for next week.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest

We received 836 photos from 208 photographers in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest. We are so thankful for the photographers who shared their time and talent in sharing their photos with us. This 24th edition of the Blue Ridge Calendar would not be possible without your generosity.

Tuesday, our judges meet to start the process of selecting around 20 finalists in each season. It's a tough job because of the ever-increasing quality of the photos we receive. We hope to have finalists selected and voting started by Thursday.

WWW... Warmer, Windy Wednesday

Last Updated Wed April 22 at 10:16AM by Ray Russell

We are in a stiff WNW flow today. Windy conditions (especially through early afternoon) are the main takeaway from today's weather, but temperatures are subtly warmer. Windy conditions combined with our ongoing drought make this the most dangerous day we have had for wildfires—caution is required. Thursday and Friday are pleasant—the picks of the week weather-wise. An approaching front brings showers Saturday afternoon into the evening.

This week's Monday Message addresses the temperature roller-coaster, drought status, and improving rain prospects for next week.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest

We received 836 photos from 208 photographers in the 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar Photo Contest. We are so thankful for the photographers who shared their time and talent in sharing their photos with us. This 24th edition of the Blue Ridge Calendar would not be possible without your generosity.

Tuesday, our judges meet to start the process of selecting around 20 finalists in each season. It's a tough job because of the ever-increasing quality of the photos we receive. We hope to have finalists selected and voting started by Thursday.

Forecast Discussion

A warm front sits to our northeast, with a weak upper-level disturbance moving through in a strong NW flow. The result today is somewhat warmer temperatures and windy conditions, especially through early afternoon.

Thursday and Friday, high pressure in the western Atlantic gets a better grip on our weather, producing a warmer SW flow aloft. These are the picks of the week.

A cold front moves through from the west Saturday evening. This front stalls just to our south, then returns as a warm front Monday night.

Get your outdoor stuff done Saturday morning before scattered showers develop in the afternoon. Showers taper off early in the evening. I nervously kept Monday daytime dry ahead of the front’s return, with scattered showers Monday night. A shower could linger into Tuesday morning.

Maybe (just maybe) we get into a somewhat rainier pattern in the second half of next week. We need significant rain badly. We have been lucky with wildfires overall so far—that luck will run out if we don’t get rain soon.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
